text.title: Health Checks + Auto-Heal Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of `Health Checks + Auto-Heal` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Health endpoint MUST exercise a real dependency (DB ping + cache ping + critical lib import), not just return 200. Synthetic-200 = REJECT.
rule.rationale: A `/health` that returns 200 without touching DB lies; service appears healthy while Postgres is unreachable.

rule.statement: Every long-running service unit MUST declare `Restart=on-failure` AND `OnFailure=service-failed@%i.service` for escalation. Missing OnFailure = REJECT.
rule.rationale: Restart=on-failure handles process crashes; OnFailure escalates to the alerting service after N retries.

rule.statement: Auto-heal loop MUST retry up to N (e.g. 3) before escalating to Telegram. Infinite retry = REJECT.
rule.rationale: Infinite restart on a broken deploy creates a restart storm that hides the root cause; bounded retries make failure visible.

rule.statement: Health-check script MUST be silent on success (no log spam, no Telegram). Inverted = REJECT.
rule.rationale: Success-spam trains operators to ignore the channel; real failures get lost.

rule.statement: Every health-check curl MUST have `--max-time 5` (or equivalent). Unbounded = REJECT.
rule.rationale: Unbounded curl hangs forever on a stuck service; the health loop itself wedges.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume cycles and produce false confidence.
